title: cymaria dichotoma
intro: Cymaria dichotoma, also known as the "bitter almond tree" or "black bitter almond", is a type of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Cymaria dichotoma are attributed to its fruit, which contains a compound called amygdalin, a natural source of benzaldehyde and hydrocyanic acid. This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including treating digestive issues, fever reduction, and as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Cymaria dichotoma is often used in the form of dried fruit, tincture, or essential oil, and is commonly used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
